message:
feat(wasm): make package discovery mandatory instead of skippable (3qv04.4.4)

Remove the "controlled skip" posture for missing package manifests and
enforce mandatory discovery in the release lane:

- Update strategy doc: missing manifests are now a hard release-blocking
  failure, not an allowed skip
- Add enforcement_mode: "mandatory" to the package policy JSON
- Add 4 contract tests: policy enforcement mode, required package
  manifest existence, no undiscovered packages, strategy doc language
- Update supply chain test to match new mandatory language

32 tests in wasm_package_build_contract.rs, 7 in wasm_supply_chain_controls.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,7 @@
 {
   "schema_version": "wasm-typescript-package-policy-v1",
   "description": "Deterministic TypeScript package topology and boundary contract for Browser Edition.",
+  "enforcement_mode": "mandatory",
   "required_packages": [
     "@asupersync/browser-core",
     "@asupersync/browser",

@@ -179,8 +179,9 @@ Policy wiring expectations:
    - assumptions artifact: `artifacts/npm/npm_release_assumptions.json`
    - publish outcome artifact: `artifacts/npm/publish_outcome.json`
    - rollback outcome artifact (when rollback mode is used): `artifacts/npm/rollback_outcome.json`
-4. Missing package manifests are treated as an explicit controlled skip
-   (artifactized) rather than an implicit silent bypass.
+4. Missing package manifests are a hard release-blocking failure. All four
+   required packages must be discovered and validated before any channel
+   promotion can proceed.
 5. Rollback mode requires both target version and operator reason; the executed
    dist-tag commands must be captured in release artifacts.
